title: Rapper Flavor Flav donates sports equipment to Cambridge youth center
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Flavor Flav donated sports equipment to Frisoli Youth Center in Cambridge, in partnership with Harvard College and Franklin Sports.
The donation included basketballs, dodgeballs, volleyballs, soccer balls, nets, tennis balls, kickballs, and cornhole equipment.
Frisoli Youth Center offers programs for children in 4th-8th grade and teens aged 14-19, fostering community and physical activity.

title: Jaylen Brown has a candid answer when asked if he deserves NBA MVP this season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown averages 28.8 points, 7.0 rebounds, and 5.2 assists per game for the Celtics in the 2025-26 NBA season.
Brown expressed humility about his MVP candidacy, stating analytics may not favor him despite his significant contributions.
Celtics are 54-26, second in the Eastern Conference; NBA MVP favorites are Shai Gilgeous-Alexander, Victor Wembanyama, Nikola Jokic.

title: Special World Cup ticket sales shatter Commuter Rail records in first day
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: More than 17,450 special World Cup train tickets sold for Gillette Stadium matches on first day, breaking MBTA records.
Nearly 11,000 tickets sold in first hour; unprecedented service planned for up to 20,000 riders per match on 14 trains.
Boston Stadium tickets allow unlimited Commuter Rail travel on match days, enhancing flexibility for World Cup attendees.

title: The Boston Fleet are ready to take a sold-out TD Garden by storm
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Fleet will play their first sold-out game at TD Garden against Montreal Victoire on Saturday.
Olympic gold medalists Megan Keller, Aerin Frankel, and Haley Winn were recently honored at a Bruins game in Boston.
The PWHL is experiencing record attendance with over 17,800 expected at TD Garden, following a record at Madison Square Garden.

title: Fall River woman who led sex trafficking ring with 5 others pleads guilty
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Christy Parker, 28, of Fall River, pleaded guilty to leading a sex trafficking ring that involved two minors and one adult.
The trafficking operation ran from January to August 2023 across Massachusetts, Rhode Island, and South Carolina.
Parker faces up to life in prison for charges including conspiracy and sex trafficking by force, fraud, and coercion.

title: Police look to identify owners of 2 dogs found dead on South Shore
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Two dead dogs were found in Weymouth, one on Whitmans Pond's shoreline and another on Lakeshore Road.
The first dog, a small white terrier mix, had no identification but wore a flea collar; its death is not suspicious.
A medium-sized Pitbull mix found wrapped in a plastic bag is considered suspicious; police seek public information.

title: She’s running Boston in memory of her son, and for  necrotizing enterocolitis
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Jennifer Canvasser will run the 2026 Boston Marathon in memory of her late son Micah and for the NEC Society.
Canvasser founded the NEC Society after Micah passed away from necrotizing enterocolitis before his first birthday.
Post-Marathon, Canvasser will present on NEC care at the Pediatric Academic Societies Meeting in Boston.

title: Braintree man sentenced to life in prison for murdering Salem State student 40 years ago
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: John Carey, 66, sentenced to life without parole for the 1986 murder of Salem State student Claire Gravel.
Carey was found guilty of first-degree murder on March 3, showing extreme atrocity and cruelty in the act.
DNA evidence and investigative breaks in 2012 led to Carey's indictment for the decades-old cold case.

title: ‘It’s pure excitement’: What James Hagens said after first practice with Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens, 19, has signed an entry-level contract with the Boston Bruins and could debut Saturday.
Head coach Marco Sturm emphasized patience, saying Hagens needs time to acclimate before debuting in high-stakes games.
Hagens is expected to improve the Bruins' power play and boost their offensive capabilities with dynamic playmaking.

title: Report: 76ers star Joel Embiid to undergo surgery for appendicitis
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Philadelphia 76ers center Joel Embiid will undergo surgery for appendicitis, missing an undetermined number of games.
Embiid's absence impacts the 76ers' playoff positioning as they aim to avoid the play-in tournament.
Embiid averages 26.9 points, 7.7 rebounds, and 3.9 assists per game this season across 38 appearances.
